channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Heidelberg Laureate Forum 2026 Journalist Travel Grant (Fully Funded to Germany)
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The Heidelberg Laureate Forum Foundation is offering an exceptional opportunity for science and technology journalists worldwide to attend the 13th Heidelberg Laureate Forum (HLF) in Germany from September 13–18, 2026. This prestigious event gathers winners of the Fields Medal, Turing Award, and Abel Prize, along with 200 of the world’s most outstanding young researchers in […]

The post Heidelberg Laureate Forum 2026 Journalist Travel Grant (Fully Funded to Germany) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Heidelberg Laureate Forum Foundation is offering an exceptional opportunity for science and technology journalists worldwide to attend the 13th Heidelberg Laureate Forum (HLF) in Germany from September 13–18, 2026.

This prestigious event gathers winners of the Fields Medal, Turing Award, and Abel Prize, along with 200 of the world’s most outstanding young researchers in mathematics and computer science. It provides a unique platform for journalists to engage directly with leading experts shaping the future of science.

🌟 About the Heidelberg Laureate Forum

The Heidelberg Laureate Forum is a globally recognized networking conference that fosters dialogue between top-tier researchers and laureates in mathematics and computer science.

Journalists selected for the program gain exclusive access to:

  • In-depth discussions on cutting-edge research

  • Direct interactions with globally recognized laureates

  • Insights into emerging scientific trends

  • A vibrant international network of experts and communicators

🎤 Special Highlight

A key highlight of the 2026 forum is the Lindau Lecture, which will be delivered by Nobel Prize-winning physicist Brian Schmidt, renowned for discovering the accelerating expansion of the universe.

💰 Travel Grant Benefits

Successful applicants will receive full financial support, including:

  • Round-trip travel to Germany

  • Accommodation throughout the forum

  • Visa costs fully covered

  • Full access to all sessions and networking opportunities

This ensures participants can focus entirely on storytelling, learning, and professional growth.

📝 Application Process

To apply for the travel grant:

  1. Create an account on the official application portal

  2. Enter your personal details (name, email, login credentials)

  3. Activate your account via the confirmation email

  4. Log in and complete your application form

  5. Submit before the deadline

Ensure your application clearly demonstrates your experience in science or technology journalism and your motivation for attending.

⏰ Application Deadline

The application period runs from March 2 to April 8, 2026.

Final Deadline: April 8, 2026

Applications submitted after this date will not be considered.

🚀 Why You Should Apply

This opportunity offers immense value for journalists looking to elevate their careers:

  • Access to exclusive, high-level scientific discussions

  • Opportunities to produce impactful global stories

  • Networking with top scientists and fellow journalists

  • Exposure to groundbreaking developments in math and computer science

🔗 How to Apply

Applicants should visit the official Heidelberg Laureate Forum website, create an account, and complete the application before the deadline.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more travel grant opportunities, click here. 

The post Heidelberg Laureate Forum 2026 Journalist Travel Grant (Fully Funded to Germany) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UN Women JPO Programme Analyst Role in Malawi (Sponsored by Finland) – Launch Your Global Career in Gender Equality
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Are you passionate about advancing gender equality and empowering women worldwide? The Government of Finland is offering an incredible opportunity for young professionals to join UN Women as a Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme Analyst in Lilongwe, Malawi. This prestigious role places you at the forefront of global development work, helping shape impactful programmes that […]

The post UN Women JPO Programme Analyst Role in Malawi (Sponsored by Finland) – Launch Your Global Career in Gender Equality appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about advancing gender equality and empowering women worldwide? The Government of Finland is offering an incredible opportunity for young professionals to join UN Women as a Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme Analyst in Lilongwe, Malawi. This prestigious role places you at the forefront of global development work, helping shape impactful programmes that uplift women economically and socially.

📌 About the Opportunity

The UN Women JPO Programme Analyst (P2 level) position focuses on Women’s Economic Empowerment (WEE), a key priority area in Malawi. As part of this role, you will contribute to designing, implementing, and evaluating programmes that promote income security, decent work, and economic autonomy for women.

Working closely with UN Women teams, government stakeholders, and international partners, you’ll help drive initiatives in areas such as:

  • Entrepreneurship and skills development

  • Digital inclusion and ICT for women

  • Agricultural value chains and employment creation

  • Policy development and gender equality frameworks

This is a full-time, fixed-term position for 1 year, with the possibility of extension.

📍 Location

Lilongwe, Malawi

🎯 Key Responsibilities

As a Programme Analyst, you will:

  • Support programme design, implementation, and evaluation

  • Monitor results using Results-Based Management tools

  • Provide technical assistance and capacity building to partners

  • Coordinate programme activities and partnerships

  • Contribute to reporting, budgeting, and donor engagement

  • Support advocacy and knowledge-building initiatives

🎓 Eligibility Criteria

To be considered, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Master’s degree in a relevant field (e.g., economics, social sciences, gender studies, international development)

  • At least 2 years of relevant professional experience

  • Strong analytical, communication, and programme management skills

  • Fluency in English (additional UN languages are an asset)

🌐 Why This Opportunity Matters

Malawi presents a unique development context where women make up over half the population and contribute significantly to agriculture and the workforce—yet face systemic barriers in land ownership, finance, and leadership.

By joining this programme, you will:

  • Gain hands-on international development experience

  • Work on impactful gender equality initiatives

  • Build a global professional network

  • Enhance your leadership and policy analysis skills

📅 Application Deadline

April 16, 2026 (23:59 CET)

🔗 Application Link

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more job opportunities, click here. 

🚀 Final Thoughts

This JPO position is more than just a job—it’s a gateway to a meaningful international career. If you are driven by purpose and ready to make a difference in women’s lives globally, this is your chance to step into a transformative role with UN Women.

The post UN Women JPO Programme Analyst Role in Malawi (Sponsored by Finland) – Launch Your Global Career in Gender Equality appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for WHO Climate and SRH Research Grant — Up to $55,000 for Community-Based Projects
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Are you part of a research team working at the intersection of climate change and public health? The World Health Organization (WHO), through its Human Reproduction Programme (HRP), is inviting applications for a global call for research proposals focused on climate change and sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRH). This opportunity supports research teams […]

The post Apply for WHO Climate and SRH Research Grant — Up to $55,000 for Community-Based Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you part of a research team working at the intersection of climate change and public health? The World Health Organization (WHO), through its Human Reproduction Programme (HRP), is inviting applications for a global call for research proposals focused on climate change and sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRH).

This opportunity supports research teams in low- and middle-income countries to generate community-driven evidence on how climate change impacts SRH outcomes. With funding of up to USD 55,000 per project, this call is designed to strengthen research capacity while addressing urgent global health challenges through participatory and interdisciplinary approaches.

About the Programme

This call for proposals focuses on understanding how climate-related events — such as droughts, floods, and extreme temperatures — affect key areas of sexual and reproductive health and rights. These include maternal health, gender-based violence, access to contraception, and abortion care.

Selected research teams will participate in a collaborative, cross-country research initiative. Rather than submitting a finalized protocol, applicants are asked to propose a research vision that demonstrates their approach, expertise, and connection to the communities they aim to study.

A central component of the programme is a Protocol Development Workshop, scheduled to take place in Geneva in July 2026. During this workshop, selected teams will co-design a unified research framework and methodology. Travel and accommodation costs for up to two team members will be covered by WHO.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Climate change is increasingly recognized as a major global health challenge, yet its impact on sexual and reproductive health remains under-researched. This call aims to address critical evidence gaps while empowering local researchers and communities.

By supporting participatory, community-led research, the programme ensures that those most affected by climate change play an active role in shaping solutions. This approach not only improves research quality but also enhances policy relevance and real-world impact.

For research teams, this opportunity offers a pathway to contribute to global knowledge, influence policy, and strengthen long-term research capacity.

What Participants Will Gain

This opportunity provides both financial and institutional support to research teams working on critical global health issues.

Selected teams will receive up to USD 55,000 in research funding, which can be used to support personnel, community engagement activities, data collection, and dissemination efforts.

Beyond funding, participants will gain access to capacity-building resources, including training, technical guidance, and research tools provided by WHO. This support helps strengthen research design, implementation, and analysis capabilities.

Teams will also benefit from international collaboration, working alongside other selected research groups across different countries. This cross-country exchange allows for shared learning, comparative analysis, and broader research impact.

Another key benefit is access to WHO expertise and networks. Selected teams will receive guidance on ethics approval processes, data collection strategies, and dissemination, as well as connections to WHO country offices.

Additionally, the programme supports participatory research approaches, meaning community members are actively involved as co-researchers. This enhances the relevance and impact of the research while strengthening community engagement.

Who Can Apply

This call is open to research teams based in low- and middle-income countries, particularly those in priority countries identified by WHO.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Academic and research institutions
  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
  • Community-based organizations and civil society groups
  • Multi-institutional research consortia

The Principal Investigator and core research team must be based in an eligible country. While international collaboration is encouraged, high-income country partners cannot lead the project and may receive no more than 15% of the total grant.

Teams should demonstrate:

  • Experience in SRH or climate-related research
  • Expertise in participatory and mixed-methods approaches
  • Strong community partnerships
  • Capacity to conduct interdisciplinary research

Research Focus Areas

Applicants must select one of four priority research questions:

  • Maternal health outcomes in the context of climate change
  • Gender-based violence during climate-related events
  • Access to contraception during climate shocks
  • Access to abortion care in climate-affected settings

All proposals must apply a human rights-based and intersectional approach, considering how factors such as gender, socioeconomic status, and migration influence vulnerability.

Application Process and Deadline

Applicants must submit their proposals through the official online platform, including all required documents such as budget, CVs, and conflict of interest declarations.

Application Deadline: 12 April 2026 (23:59 GMT+1)

Late or incomplete applications will not be accepted. Selected teams will be notified by 11 May 2026.

 Learn more and apply through the official WHO/HRP application platform.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply for WHO Climate and SRH Research Grant — Up to $55,000 for Community-Based Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: United Nations IFAD Internship Programme(Paid Internship open to various nationalities)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is an international financial institution and a specialized United Nations agency dedicated to eradicating rural poverty and hunger. It does so by investing in rural people. IFAD finances programmes and projects that increase agricultural productivity and raise rural incomes, and advocates at the local, national and international level […]

The post United Nations IFAD Internship Programme(Paid Internship open to various nationalities) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is an international financial institution and a specialized United Nations agency dedicated to eradicating rural poverty and hunger. It does so by investing in rural people. IFAD finances programmes and projects that increase agricultural productivity and raise rural incomes, and advocates at the local, national and international level for policies that contribute to rural transformation.

Job Role

The regular Internship Programme (IP) and Enhanced Internship Programme (EIP) offer a unique learning experience of six months to recent university graduates or students who are currently specializing in areas of work relevant to IFAD’s mission. Candidates for the Internship Programme must be 30 years old or less and must be enrolled in an accredited university or graduate school, have attended courses in the last 12 months, and have completed at least two years of undergraduate studies when joining the programme.

Candidates must meet the following criteria:

  • be enrolled in a university or graduate school, have attended courses in the last 12 months, and have completed at least two years of undergraduate studies when joining IFAD through the programme or;
  • have recently completed university studies at the undergraduate or postgraduate level;
  • be fluent in English; fluency in other IFAD official languages is an asset. In the case of interns recruited in IFAD country office, multi country office or regional office locations, fluency in the main country language may be required;
  • be 30 years of age or younger. The Enhanced Internship Programme, in particular, is designed to enhance geographic distribution of staff at IFAD and is open to candidates who meet the above eligibility criteria and who are not based in Rome.

Benefits include:

  • Monthly stipend
  • Housing and travel allowances (Enhanced Internship Programme)
  • Mentorship, learning, and growth opportunities in a dynamic, global environment

Financial support: In addition to a monthly allowance, Enhanced Internship Programme interns receive a housing and travel allowance.

To apply, please login or register as an intern in IFAD’s applicant system. Then click on ‘View all jobs’ and select the option under Job Opening Type, Internship Programme. For more info visit: https://www.ifad.org/en/internship-programme 
FAQS
You may also be interested in this : Click here for Geospatial Analysis Internship at IFAD in Rome 

The post United Nations IFAD Internship Programme(Paid Internship open to various nationalities) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: PAYA Youth Council 2026: Paid Leadership Opportunity for Youth Apprentices in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the PAYA Youth Council, a paid leadership program under the Partnership to Advance Youth Apprenticeship (PAYA). This unique opportunity is designed for current and former youth apprentices who are passionate about advocacy, leadership, and expanding access to apprenticeship opportunities across the United States. Participants will gain hands-on experience leading projects, […]

The post PAYA Youth Council 2026: Paid Leadership Opportunity for Youth Apprentices in the U.S. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the PAYA Youth Council, a paid leadership program under the Partnership to Advance Youth Apprenticeship (PAYA).

This unique opportunity is designed for current and former youth apprentices who are passionate about advocacy, leadership, and expanding access to apprenticeship opportunities across the United States.

Participants will gain hands-on experience leading projects, influencing policy conversations, and working alongside peers from across the country—all while receiving a stipend.

About the PAYA Youth Council

The PAYA Youth Council is a leadership development initiative that empowers young people to take an active role in shaping the future of apprenticeship programs.

Council members collaborate on youth-led projects, contribute to national conversations about workforce development, and advocate for improved access to career pathways.

The program also creates a strong sense of community, bringing together young leaders committed to making a difference.

What You Will Do

As a member of the Youth Council, participants will:

Develop advocacy and leadership skills
Lead and contribute to youth-driven projects
Engage in discussions on apprenticeship and workforce development
Collaborate with peers from across the United States
Represent youth voices in national conversations

Participants will also have opportunities to share their ideas and create impact through real-world initiatives.

Program Benefits

The PAYA Youth Council offers both professional development and financial support.

Benefits include:

A generous stipend for participation
Hands-on leadership and advocacy experience
Networking opportunities with peers and professionals
Participation in a summer celebration event in Washington, D.C.
Exposure to national apprenticeship initiatives

This is a valuable opportunity for young people looking to build leadership experience while contributing to meaningful change.

Why You Should Apply

If you are passionate about youth empowerment, workforce development, and creating opportunities for others, this program provides a platform to make your voice heard.

Past participants have highlighted the impact of seeing their work contribute to real change and helping others access opportunities.

Additional Opportunities

Interested applicants can also:

Attend an upcoming virtual info session to learn more about the program
Explore past Youth Council projects
Hear directly from former members about their experiences

These resources provide insight into what to expect and how to make the most of the opportunity.

How to Apply

Apply here

Info session

Past projects

Hear from past members

For more Fellowship opportunities, Visit OFY Here.

The post PAYA Youth Council 2026: Paid Leadership Opportunity for Youth Apprentices in the U.S. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: MSc in Public Policy and Human Development 2026: Study Policy, Development, and Global Change in the Netherlands(Scholarship Available)
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Master of Science in Public Policy and Human Development (MPP), a prestigious one-year postgraduate programme offered through a collaboration between United Nations University (UNU-MERIT) and Maastricht University. Delivered in Maastricht, Netherlands, this internationally recognized programme is designed for individuals who want to tackle complex global challenges and contribute to […]

The post MSc in Public Policy and Human Development 2026: Study Policy, Development, and Global Change in the Netherlands(Scholarship Available) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Master of Science in Public Policy and Human Development (MPP), a prestigious one-year postgraduate programme offered through a collaboration between United Nations University (UNU-MERIT) and Maastricht University.

Delivered in Maastricht, Netherlands, this internationally recognized programme is designed for individuals who want to tackle complex global challenges and contribute to shaping effective public policies aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals.

About the Programme

The MSc in Public Policy and Human Development is an intensive, full-time programme that runs for one year and awards two diplomas—one from Maastricht University and one from the United Nations University.

The programme combines theoretical knowledge with practical skills, preparing students to design, implement, monitor, and evaluate public policies across sectors.

With a strong focus on real-world impact, students gain both quantitative and qualitative skills needed to influence policy decisions at local, national, and global levels.

Programme Structure

The programme is divided into two main phases:

Autumn Semester
Focuses on quantitative skills such as statistics, regression analysis, and the use of tools like STATA. This phase equips students with analytical skills essential for policy research.

Spring Semester
Centers on specialization and thesis writing, allowing students to tailor their learning based on their interests.

Students can choose from four specialization tracks:

Social Protection Policy
Migration Studies
Governance of Innovation
Global Governance for Development

The programme uses Problem-Based Learning, encouraging critical thinking, collaboration, and active participation.

What You Will Learn

Participants will develop the ability to:

Analyze complex societal challenges
Design and evaluate effective public policies
Apply data-driven approaches to decision-making
Develop solutions aligned with sustainable development goals
Communicate policy recommendations effectively

The programme also includes workshops, simulations, debates, and networking events with global experts.

Programme Benefits

Students benefit from a highly international and personalized learning experience.

Key advantages include:

Dual degree from two globally recognized institutions
Small class sizes and personalized academic support
Access to global networks and policy experts
A dedicated employability track with mentoring and career support
Strong career prospects in international organizations, governments, NGOs, and private sector institutions

Graduates have gone on to work with organizations such as international development banks, UN agencies, consulting firms, and government ministries.

Scholarships and Tuition

Tuition fees for the 2026/2027 academic year are:

Statutory fee: €2,695
Institutional fee: €21,500

The programme offers financial support options, including the MPP Scholarship, which covers the full institutional tuition fee for one outstanding non-EU applicant.

Applicants are encouraged to explore additional funding options such as the Commonwealth Scholarship.

Programme Details

Programme start date: September 1, 2026
Duration: 1 year (full-time)
Location: Maastricht, Netherlands

Application Deadlines

Non-EU/EEA applicants (requiring a visa): May 1, 2026
EU applicants: June 1, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early to secure admission and explore scholarship opportunities.

Who Should Apply

The programme welcomes applicants from diverse academic and professional backgrounds, including recent graduates and mid-career professionals.

It is ideal for individuals interested in:

Public policy
International development
Governance and global affairs
Economic and social policy

How to Apply

Interested candidates can learn more about the programme and submit their application through the official Maastricht University portal.

Prospective students are advised to carefully review admission requirements and prepare all necessary documents before applying.

For more Scholarship opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post MSc in Public Policy and Human Development 2026: Study Policy, Development, and Global Change in the Netherlands(Scholarship Available) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Women’s Impact Award 2026: Global Call for Women Researchers Driving Scientific Excellence and Societal Impact
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Women’s Impact Award 2026, presented by the Falling Walls Foundation in collaboration with the Elsevier Foundation, is a prestigious global recognition designed to celebrate outstanding women researchers whose work combines scientific excellence with measurable societal impact. As part of the broader Global Call for Science Breakthroughs 2026, this award highlights transformative research that addresses […]

The post Women’s Impact Award 2026: Global Call for Women Researchers Driving Scientific Excellence and Societal Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Women’s Impact Award 2026, presented by the Falling Walls Foundation in collaboration with the Elsevier Foundation, is a prestigious global recognition designed to celebrate outstanding women researchers whose work combines scientific excellence with measurable societal impact.

As part of the broader Global Call for Science Breakthroughs 2026, this award highlights transformative research that addresses pressing global challenges while promoting equity, inclusion, and sustainable development. It is an opportunity for leading women scientists and researchers worldwide to gain international recognition, expand their influence, and contribute to shaping a more equitable future.

About the Women’s Impact Award

The Women’s Impact Award is a flagship initiative that honors women who are not only advancing scientific knowledge but also applying their research to create tangible, real-world change.

The award specifically recognizes:

  • Scientific excellence at the highest level

  • Research with clear societal relevance and impact

  • Contributions that advance equity, inclusion, and justice

  • Leadership in addressing global and community-level challenges

This initiative is part of the Falling Walls Foundation’s mission to identify and celebrate breakthroughs in science that have the potential to change the world.

About the Global Call for Science Breakthroughs 2026

The Global Call for Science Breakthroughs is an international platform that invites nominations and applications from leading researchers across disciplines. It provides a stage for groundbreaking work that pushes the boundaries of knowledge and contributes to solving critical global issues.

The Women’s Impact Award is a dedicated category within this global call, focusing specifically on women and gender-diverse researchers.

What the Award Recognizes

The Women’s Impact Award celebrates research projects that combine academic rigor with societal relevance. Applicants must demonstrate that their work contributes meaningfully to one or more of the following areas:

1. Advancing Gender Equality through Research

Eligible projects may include:

  • Research addressing gender bias in fields such as artificial intelligence, healthcare, education, finance, or employment

  • Studies that incorporate sex and gender differences in data collection, analysis, and interpretation

  • Projects using gender-sensitive methodologies, including:

    • Gender-disaggregated data

    • Co-design approaches involving women and gender-diverse populations

  • Interdisciplinary research that promotes inclusive innovation

2. Supporting Underserved and Marginalized Communities

Applicants may also qualify if their research focuses on empowering underserved populations through:

  • Participatory research involving communities such as:

    • Refugees

    • Rural populations

    • People with disabilities

    • Low-income groups

  • Development of technologies, policies, or interventions that improve:

    • Access to healthcare

    • Educational opportunities

    • Justice and legal systems

    • Safety and protection

    • Economic inclusion

  • Research outcomes that actively reduce inequality or dismantle systemic barriers

Projects are not required to focus exclusively on women or girls, but they must clearly demonstrate a commitment to equity, inclusion, and positive societal transformation.

Eligibility Criteria

To be considered for the Women’s Impact Award 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements:

1. Career Level

Applicants must be at:

  • Postdoctoral level or above, or

  • An equivalent level of professional independence and responsibility

Eligible roles include:

  • Postdoctoral researchers

  • Research fellows

  • Senior researchers

  • Principal investigators

  • Group leaders

  • Professors

2. Inclusive Eligibility

The award embraces diversity and inclusivity. Applications are welcome from:

  • Cisgender women

  • Transgender women

  • Genderqueer individuals

  • Non-binary individuals

This inclusive approach ensures representation across diverse gender identities within the scientific community.

Research Project Requirements

Applicants must be actively engaged in a research project at the time of submission.

Eligible Project Stages:

  • Data collection or analysis

  • Pilot or testing phases

  • Implementation in real-world settings

Ineligible Projects:

  • Projects at the idea stage

  • Concepts under development

  • Proposals seeking funding without active implementation

Language Requirement:

  • All applications must be submitted in English

Who Can Nominate

Nominations for the Women’s Impact Award 2026 can be submitted by a wide range of institutions and individuals, including:

  • Universities and higher education institutions

  • Research organizations and institutes

  • Companies with research laboratories

  • Scientific foundations

  • Partner organizations of the Falling Walls Foundation

  • Leading scientists and experts in relevant fields

Applicants may also apply directly, depending on the nomination pathway.

Benefits of the Women’s Impact Award

While specific prize details may vary, recipients of the Women’s Impact Award typically benefit from:

  • Global recognition at a prestigious international platform

  • Visibility among leading scientists, policymakers, and innovators

  • Opportunities to expand professional networks

  • Increased credibility and influence in their field

  • Access to future collaborations and funding opportunities

Notable Past Winners

The Women’s Impact Award has previously recognized exceptional researchers whose work has made significant contributions to science and society.

Some recent awardees include:

  • Ângela Gonçalves (German Cancer Research Center)

  • Colette Wabnitz (Stanford Center for Ocean Solutions; University of British Columbia)

  • Omneya Attallah (Arab Academy for Science, Technology, and Maritime Transport)

These winners exemplify the caliber of research and impact the award seeks to celebrate.

Application Process

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply or be nominated through the Official Global Call for Science Breakthroughs platform.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Prepare detailed information about your research project

  2. Demonstrate scientific excellence and societal impact

  3. Submit your application in English

  4. Ensure your project meets all eligibility criteria

Application Deadline: 15-Apr-2026

Why You Should Apply

The Women’s Impact Award 2026 offers a unique opportunity to:

  • Showcase your research on a global stage

  • Gain recognition for impactful scientific work

  • Contribute to advancing gender equality and inclusion

  • Join a network of leading researchers and innovators

  • Strengthen your academic and professional profile

Learn more about the Women’s Impact Award 2026 HERE.

Click HERE to discover more global Award opportunities on OFY.

The post Women’s Impact Award 2026: Global Call for Women Researchers Driving Scientific Excellence and Societal Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 (Fully Funded): Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
If you are a young leader passionate about creating real change in your community, the Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 offers one of the most prestigious fully funded opportunities to gain global exposure, leadership training, and international recognition. This scholarship, offered by One Young World in partnership with the Capital City of Podgorica, provides […]

The post Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 (Fully Funded): Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are a young leader passionate about creating real change in your community, the Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 offers one of the most prestigious fully funded opportunities to gain global exposure, leadership training, and international recognition. This scholarship, offered by One Young World in partnership with the Capital City of Podgorica, provides a unique pathway to attend the globally acclaimed One Young World Summit 2026.

About the Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026

The Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship is a highly competitive, fully funded opportunity designed to support one exceptional young leader from Podgorica to participate in a global leadership experience.

Podgorica, one of Europe’s youngest capitals and a future European Youth Capital 2028, is actively investing in youth empowerment, innovation, and civic participation.

Through this initiative, selected scholars will join thousands of global changemakers at the One Young World Summit, where leaders from over 190 countries gather to address pressing global challenges such as:

  • Climate change and environmental protection

  • Social inequality and education

  • Youth participation and governance

  • Entrepreneurship and innovation

What Makes the One Young World Summit So Valuable?

The One Young World Summit 2026 is one of the most influential global gatherings of young leaders. It brings together:

  • Entrepreneurs and innovators

  • Activists and policy advocates

  • Students and professionals

  • Social impact leaders

Participants gain access to world-class mentorship, networking opportunities, and leadership development programs that can transform their careers and amplify their impact globally.

Scholarship Benefits (Fully Funded Package)

The Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 provides comprehensive financial and professional support, including:

1. Full Access to the Global Summit – Participation in all sessions of the One Young World Summit 2026

2. Travel Coverage – Round-trip international flights (economy class)

3. Accommodation – Hotel stay from 2 November to 7 November 2026

4. Meals and Catering

  • Breakfast at the hotel

  • Lunch and dinner during the Summit

5. Local Transportation – Transport between accommodation and Summit venue

6. Visa Support – Visa application costs and related travel expenses covered

7. Leadership Development Opportunities

  • Access to the One Young World Global Leadership Programme

  • Participation in the Action Accelerator

  • Lifetime membership in the One Young World Ambassador Community

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Candidates)

This opportunity is highly specific and targeted. Applicants must meet all the following requirements:

Geographic Eligibility

  • Must be a national of Montenegro

  • Must be living and actively contributing in Podgorica

Age Requirement

  • Must be between 18 and 30 years old by November 2026

Language Requirement

  • Must have a strong working knowledge of English

Availability

  • Must be available full-time from 2–7 November 2026

Target Audience (Who This Opportunity Is For)

This scholarship is ideal for:

  • Students

  • Young professionals

  • Social entrepreneurs

  • Activists and advocates

  • Innovators and community leaders

Applicants must demonstrate a strong commitment to creating impact in areas such as:

  • Environmental sustainability

  • Youth engagement and participation

  • Cultural development

  • Digital innovation

  • Education and equality

  • Entrepreneurship

Selection Criteria (How Winners Are Chosen)

The selection process is rigorous and based on multiple impact-driven factors:

1. Proven Impact – Applicants must show measurable contributions to positive change in Podgorica or Montenegro.

2. Community Engagement – Evidence of active participation in:

  • Volunteering

  • Civic initiatives

  • Youth programs

3. Academic or Professional Excellence – Strong achievements in education, career, or extracurricular activities.

4. Vision for Future Impact – A clear plan for how the Summit experience will be used to benefit the local community.

5. Leadership Potential – Ability to represent Podgorica on a global stage and act as a youth ambassador.

6. Alignment with Global Values – Commitment to building a fair, sustainable, and inclusive future.

Important Dates

  • Applications Opened: 29 January 2026

  • Summit Dates: 3–6 November 2026

Missing the deadline automatically disqualifies your application, so early submission is strongly recommended.

Why You Should Apply

This is more than just a scholarship—it is a career-defining opportunity. Scholars gain:

  • Global recognition and credibility

  • Access to an elite international network

  • Exposure to world leaders and changemakers

  • Opportunities for future funding and collaborations

  • Long-term leadership development

According to program insights, participants return with stronger networks, enhanced skills, and greater capacity to drive impact locally and globally.

Apply here

Deadline

23rd March 2026

The Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 stands out as a highly prestigious, fully funded opportunity for young leaders committed to meaningful change. However, it is geographically restricted to Montenegro, making it extremely competitive within that pool.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 (Fully Funded): Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Vigdís Prize for Women’s Empowerment 2026: €60,000 Award for Global Leaders Advancing Gender Equality
item.dc:creator: Ankita Baidya
item.description:
The Vigdís Prize for Women’s Empowerment 2026 is a prestigious international award presented by the Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe (PACE), in partnership with the Government of Iceland. This highly respected prize recognizes and rewards outstanding individuals, organizations, and initiatives that have demonstrated exceptional commitment to advancing women’s empowerment and gender equality across […]

The post Vigdís Prize for Women’s Empowerment 2026: €60,000 Award for Global Leaders Advancing Gender Equality appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Vigdís Prize for Women’s Empowerment 2026 is a prestigious international award presented by the Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe (PACE), in partnership with the Government of Iceland. This highly respected prize recognizes and rewards outstanding individuals, organizations, and initiatives that have demonstrated exceptional commitment to advancing women’s empowerment and gender equality across the world.

Named after Vigdís Finnbogadóttir, the first woman in history to be democratically elected as a Head of State, this award celebrates transformative leadership, inclusive policies, and impactful actions that promote the full and equal participation of women and girls in all spheres of life.

With a monetary award of 60,000 euros and global recognition, the Vigdís Prize stands as one of the most significant honors dedicated to gender equality and women’s rights.

About the Vigdís Prize for Women’s Empowerment

Launched in 2023, the Vigdís Prize aims to spotlight initiatives and leaders who are making meaningful contributions toward empowering women and girls in diverse contexts.

The award specifically recognizes efforts that:

  • Advance gender equality at local, national, or global levels

  • Promote equal participation in political, social, and economic decision-making

  • Foster inclusive policies and practices

  • Address systemic barriers affecting women and girls

  • Support diversity and inclusion across all communities

At a time when gender equality continues to face challenges globally, this prize serves as a powerful platform to highlight impactful work and inspire further progress.

Prize Value and Recognition

The Vigdís Prize offers both financial and symbolic recognition to its winner:

  • A monetary award of 60,000 euros

  • A prestigious trophy

  • International recognition and visibility

  • Opportunity to present work on a global stage

The award ceremony takes place in Strasbourg, France, during the Summer Session of the Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe.

Key Dates for 2026

Applicants and nominators should take note of the following timeline:

  • 21 November 2025: Call for candidates opened

  • 27 March 2026: Deadline for submission of nominations

  • May 2026: First meeting of the Selection Panel in Reykjavik

  • 21 May 2026: Shortlisting of three candidates

  • 21 June 2026: Final selection of the winner in Strasbourg

  • 22 June 2026: Official award ceremony

Who Can Be Nominated

The Vigdís Prize is open to a wide range of nominees who are actively contributing to women’s empowerment.

Eligible nominees include:

  • Individuals

  • Civil society organizations

  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)

  • Associations

  • Private companies

Both national and international candidates are eligible, reflecting the global scope of the award.

Areas of Impact Recognized

Nominees should demonstrate significant achievements in one or more of the following areas:

1. Advancing Gender Equality

  • Promoting equal rights and opportunities for women and girls

  • Addressing discrimination and gender-based inequalities

  • Supporting inclusive governance and leadership

2. Empowering Women and Girls

  • Initiatives that enhance access to education, healthcare, and economic opportunities

  • Programs that support women’s leadership and participation

  • Actions that protect women’s rights and dignity

3. Promoting Inclusive Participation

  • Encouraging women’s involvement in decision-making processes

  • Creating platforms for marginalized voices

  • Strengthening representation across sectors

Nomination Requirements

To successfully submit a nomination for the Vigdís Prize 2026, the following requirements must be met:

1. Nomination Form

  • Complete the Official Nomination Form

  • Provide detailed information about the nominee and their work

2. Language

  • Applications must be submitted in either English or French

3. Sponsorship Requirement

  • Each nomination must be supported by at least five sponsors

  • Sponsors must provide their signatures using the official sponsors’ form

4. Submission Format

  • All documents must be compiled into a single PDF file

  • File naming format:

    • For organizations: NAME – Country

    • For individuals: FAMILY NAME – First Name – Country

5. Submission Process

  • Upload the complete application package through the official submission platform

Selection Process

The selection of the Vigdís Prize winner is carried out by an independent Selection Panel composed of experts in women’s empowerment.

Composition of the Panel:

  • Six independent members

  • Three nominated by the Parliamentary Assembly

  • Three nominated by the Government of Iceland

Evaluation Criteria:

  • Demonstrated impact on women’s empowerment

  • Innovation and sustainability of the initiative

  • Contribution to gender equality and inclusion

  • Measurable outcomes and long-term potential

Award Ceremony

The Vigdís Prize 2026 will be awarded during an official ceremony held in Strasbourg, France, on 22 June 2026, as part of the Summer Session of the Parliamentary Assembly.

The winner will be announced by the President of the Parliamentary Assembly, providing global recognition and visibility.

Previous Winners

The Vigdís Prize has already recognized impactful initiatives across the world:

  • 2025 Winner: Women of the Sun (Palestine) – recognized for their work in women’s rights and peacebuilding

  • 2024 Winner: Irida Women’s Center (Greece) – supporting women facing poverty, social exclusion, and gender-based violence

These examples highlight the diversity and impact of initiatives honored by the prize.

Why You Should Apply or Nominate

The Vigdís Prize offers a unique opportunity to:

  • Gain international recognition for impactful work

  • Secure financial support to expand initiatives

  • Join a global network of changemakers

  • Amplify efforts in advancing gender equality

  • Inspire others through proven impact

How to Apply

To apply or nominate a candidate:

  1. Carefully review the Official regulations

  2. Complete the Nomination form

  3. Gather at least five Sponsor signatures

  4. Compile all documents into one PDF

  5. Submit via the Official Platform before the deadline

Deadline

All nominations must be submitted by 27 March 2026.

Late submissions will not be considered.

Learn more about the Vigdís Prize for Women’s Empowerment 2026 HERE.

Click HERE for more global Award opportunities on Opportunities for Youth.

The post Vigdís Prize for Women’s Empowerment 2026: €60,000 Award for Global Leaders Advancing Gender Equality appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026 (Fully Funded): A Life-Changing Opportunity for Young Leaders in Africa, Asia and the Middle East to Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026 is one of the most prestigious fully funded global leadership opportunities designed for young changemakers who are actively transforming their communities. Offered by One Young World, this scholarship provides selected candidates with a fully funded chance to attend the globally renowned One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town. About […]

The post Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026 (Fully Funded): A Life-Changing Opportunity for Young Leaders in Africa, Asia and the Middle East to Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026 is one of the most prestigious fully funded global leadership opportunities designed for young changemakers who are actively transforming their communities. Offered by One Young World, this scholarship provides selected candidates with a fully funded chance to attend the globally renowned One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town.

About the Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship

The Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship was created to empower emerging leaders who are addressing some of the most pressing global challenges affecting women, girls, and vulnerable populations. The initiative is named after Hauwa Ojeifo, a globally recognized advocate for mental health and gender equality.

The scholarship aims to build a new generation of leaders equipped with the tools, networks, and global exposure needed to drive meaningful change in their communities. It focuses particularly on social impact areas such as mental health, gender-based violence, and reproductive health rights.

What is the One Young World Summit?

The One Young World Summit is a prestigious global event that gathers outstanding young leaders from across the world. Delegates participate in high-level discussions, networking sessions, and leadership training alongside global influencers, policymakers, and industry leaders.

Participants also gain lifetime membership in the One Young World Ambassador community, enabling long-term collaboration and impact.

Fully Funded Benefits of the Scholarship

The Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026 provides a comprehensive funding package that removes financial barriers and ensures full participation:

  • Full access to the One Young World Summit 2026

  • Round-trip airfare to Cape Town

  • Hotel accommodation for the entire duration of the summit

  • Daily meals (breakfast, lunch, and dinner)

  • Ground transportation between accommodation and event venue

  • Lifetime membership in the One Young World Ambassador network

  • Access to leadership programs and global initiatives

  • Opportunity to apply for funding of up to $25,000 for social impact projects

Fields and Focus Areas

Applicants must be actively working in at least one of the following impact areas:

  • Mental health and psychosocial support

  • Gender-based violence and domestic abuse prevention

  • Sexual and reproductive health rights

  • Maternal and child health

  • Disability rights and inclusion

  • Advocacy for the girl child (e.g., child marriage, early pregnancy)

  • Non-communicable diseases

These focus areas align with global development priorities and aim to support initiatives that create sustainable social impact.

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Categories of Applicants)

This scholarship is designed for:

  • Young leaders and changemakers

  • Social entrepreneurs and nonprofit founders

  • Community organizers and activists

  • Professionals working in public health, gender equality, or human rights

  • Individuals leading grassroots or national-level initiatives

Eligibility Criteria (Detailed Requirements)

To qualify for the Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026, applicants must meet the following criteria:

1. Age Requirement

  • Must be between 18 and 30 years old

  • Candidates above 30 may be considered if they demonstrate exceptional impact

2. Leadership and Impact

  • Must hold a leadership role or lead a project/initiative

  • Demonstrated measurable impact in their community

3. Thematic Alignment

  • Work must align with at least one of the scholarship’s focus areas

4. Geographic Eligibility

  • Applicants must live and create impact in Africa, Asia, or the Middle East

5. Language Requirement

  • Proficiency in English (spoken and written)

6. Commitment

  • Must be available to attend the summit and actively participate in all sessions

Why You Should Apply for This Scholarship

This opportunity goes beyond financial support. It offers:

  • Global exposure and recognition

  • Access to influential leaders and mentors

  • Opportunities for funding and scaling your project

  • A powerful international network of changemakers

  • Personal and professional development through leadership training

The scholarship is particularly impactful for individuals who are already making a difference but lack the global platform to amplify their work.

Apply here

Deadline

27th March 2026

The Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026 stands out as a transformative opportunity for young leaders dedicated to advancing gender equality, mental health awareness, and social justice. By removing financial barriers and connecting changemakers to a global platform, One Young World continues to empower the next generation of leaders who are shaping the future.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026 (Fully Funded): A Life-Changing Opportunity for Young Leaders in Africa, Asia and the Middle East to Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
